overview: Lightweight desktop audio plugin host built on JUCE 8.0.13. Lives in the system tray (Windows) / menu bar (macOS) with no main window; audio flows through a realtime AudioProcessorGraph that supports up to 4 parallel processing lanes with automatic Plugin Delay Compensation (PDC). Hosts VST/VST3/AU plugins. Settings — device choice, plugin chain, lane assignments, per-plugin state — persist via PropertiesFile. Current version 4.0.2. Forked from Rolando Islas (2015), GPLv2+.
graph:
HostStartup.cpp -> JUCEApplication entry; creates IconMenu in initialise()
IconMenu (.hpp/.cpp) -> central state: holds AudioDeviceManager, AudioProcessorGraph, plugin list, PropertiesFile; renders SystemTrayIconComponent + right-click PopupMenu; owns the load-active-plugins background pipeline; rebuilds the graph (reconnect()) on every chain/device/lane change with auto-PDC via DelayProcessor
IconMenu -> PreferencesWindow (left-click): unified settings UI — audio device tab + plugin chain tab (per-row lane combo)
IconMenu -> PluginWindow (per-plugin edit): single-window-per-plugin lookup keyed by node id
DelayProcessor (header-only) -> AudioProcessor that delays by N samples; auto-inserted on shorter lanes during reconnect to match max-lane latency
CMakeLists.txt + CMakePresets.json -> build config; JUCE is vendored under lib/JUCE and added via add_subdirectory; juce_add_gui_app produces 'Light Host'
.github/workflows/ci.yml -> Windows+Linux build on push/PR
.github/workflows/release.yml -> tag v* triggers parallel builds + zipped artefacts attached to a GitHub Release
